narrative:
The Zebras are positioned for a high-ceiling offensive output today, anchored by Kyle Schwarber (85.44) and Christian Yelich (81.45), both of whom benefit from favorable park factors and elite SP skill matchups. Yordan Alvarez also remains a core building block; despite a slight platoon drag, his raw base talent and favorable lineup slotting at #2 keep his projected output above the 80-point threshold. Junior Caminero rounds out the elite tier of this lineup, as his consistent order placement and strong SP matchup make him a high-efficiency play against Taillon.

Management must remain cautious regarding the lower end of the lineup, specifically Pete Crow-Armstrong (40.69) and Kazuma Okamoto (49.21), both of whom are facing significant headwinds due to SP skill deficiencies and unfavorable platoon splits. We are monitoring "Wind In" conditions that could suppress power numbers for Keith, Yelich, and Alvarez, though the projected output remains robust. Strategically, we are leaning into the BvP sample sizes for Schwarber, who holds a slight positive edge against Houser, providing a necessary stabilizer in a volatile early-season matchup.
